Job Title: Senior Software Engineer- ML & Data Pipelines
Ad Text:
Senior Software Engineer – ML & Data Pipelines

We are a growing fintech scale-up, building the next generation of data-driven financial applications. We are looking for an experienced software and data engineer to help design, develop, and scale our systems and ML pipelines.
Responsibilities

Develop, maintain, and scale software systems and data pipelines that power analytics and ML applications.
Build, deploy, and monitor ML pipelines, ensuring reliability and performance in production.
Design and implement APIs and services to integrate ML models and data workflows into production systems.
Contribute to system and data architecture, offering practical, scalable solutions for complex problems.
Apply testing frameworks and best practices to ensure robust, high-quality software.
Work closely with team members to share knowledge, provide feedback, and drive continuous improvement.
Requirements

Bachelor’s degree or higher in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.
5+ years of software development experience.
Strong experience with Python, SQL, and Linux.
Experience designing reliable, high-throughput batch or streaming systems.
Experience developing and integrating RESTful or gRPC APIs.
Practical experience with ML Ops – deploying, monitoring, and maintaining ML pipelines.
Familiarity with real-time NoSQL databases (e.g., Firestore, Bigtable, DynamoDB) is a plus.
Experience with CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions, Cloud Build, Jenkins).
Strong problem-solving skills and ability to tackle complex technical challenges.
Why this role is exciting

Join a fast-growing fintech scale-up building innovative, high-impact systems.
Collaborate with a skilled, multidisciplinary team of engineers and data specialists.
Opportunity to grow your skills across software development, data engineering, and ML Ops.

Endpoint IQ is a Brisbane-based SaaS company scaling globally, delivering industry-leading research administration platforms to universities, research institutes and health organisations.

Why this role will excite you

Shape how research runs. Your work directly cuts red tape for researchers at universities, MRIs and health services around the world.

Variety every week. One day you're configuring a solution with a client, the next you're shaping the product roadmap with our engineers.

Ship, don't stall. We move in weeks, not quarters — your ideas land in production and in front of clients fast.

Human-centred culture. We back each other, share wins, and leave egos (and suits) at the door.

The Role: As a Software Implementation Consultant, you'll operate at the intersection of project delivery, product expertise and client engagement. You'll manage multiple concurrent implementations while also getting hands-on with configuring solutions and shaping how our products evolve.

What's unique: You'll act as a trusted advisor to clients, translating complex research management needs into practical, high-impact solutions. Internally, you'll work closely with product and technical teams, contributing directly to product improvements and delivery innovation.

What You'll Do:

Own end-to-end delivery of multiple implementations, ensuring projects are on time, on budget and within scope

Act as the primary client contact, managing stakeholders and building trusted relationships

Translate client requirements into scalable system configurations aligned to best practice

Work with a global team to configure solutions and provide technical guidance on requirements

Identify and manage risks proactively, escalating where needed

Lead workshops, product demos and training to support effective implementation and adoption

Provide structured project updates and contribute to post-project reviews

Support complex client issues and triage requests in line with SLAs

Collaborate with product and technical teams to shape development priorities and solutions

We're Looking For:

Core Skills & Experience

Experience in research management is highly regarded or alternatively business analysis or product delivery

Minimum 3-5 years of professional experience in a relevant field

Proven ability to manage multiple concurrent projects and stakeholders

Strong facilitation skills, with experience running workshops and engaging senior stakeholders

Ability to translate complex requirements into practical solutions

Highly organised, detail-oriented and comfortable working in a fast-paced environment

Strong problem-solving skills with a proactive, ownership mindset

Technical & Domain Capability

Practical experience in software configuration and systems.

Understanding of research administration processes (highly regarded)

Experience working in SaaS or software delivery environments

Ability to bridge the gap between business needs and technical teams




What We Offer:

Work on meaningful projects that transform how research organisations operate

Be part of a growing global SaaS company with strong momentum

High-impact role with real influence on delivery, product, and client outcomes

Collaborative, no-silo environment where ideas turn into action

Strong focus on professional growth, capability building, and innovation

Employment type: This is a 2 years fixed-term role, with potential for extension.

Location: Toowong (Brisbane) with flexible work options

Australian citizenship is required for this position.

Ready to make your communication skills count? Apply with your CV and a Cover letter outlining your suitability for the role. Be sure to include 2–3 bullet points on how you would like to work with AI or automation for successful project delivery.

About the Role
 
uandu Recruitment Partners are delighted to partner exclusively with Murdoch Children’s Research Institute (MCRI) to recruit a number of essential positions within their Technology Office.
 
MCRI is investing heavily in its technology ecosystem to support increasingly data-rich research and clinical innovation. As part of this, they are seeking a Data Governance and Architecture specialist to take ownership of enterprise data frameworks, architecture, and governance practices across the organisation.
 
Reporting to the Head of Cybersecurity and Architecture, you will play a critical role in shaping how data is classified, secured, governed and leveraged across research and operational environments. This is a highly strategic and hands-on role, influencing everything from policy and standards through to solution design, implementation and continuous improvement.
 
About Murdoch Children’s Research Institute
 
Murdoch Children’s Research Institute envisions a transformative future for child health. Their institute is a hub of impactful scientific breakthroughs, particularly in early interventions, enhanced diagnosis, prevention and potential cures. They thrive on collaborative, multidisciplinary problem-solving to address challenging queries in child health.
 
Committed to inclusivity and respect, MCRI fosters a positive working environment that values diverse backgrounds and experiences. As a proud member of the Australian Network on Disability and a dedicated supporter of Science Australia Gender Equity (SAGE), the Institute upholds principles of equal opportunity and diverse representation.
 
MCRI is committed to the safety of children and young people and takes a zero-tolerance approach to all forms of child abuse, exploitation and harm. Child safety is prioritised in decision-making at all levels of the Institute.
 
Key Responsibilities

Define and evolve enterprise data architecture and governance frameworks across cloud, infrastructure and applications;
Lead data classification, labelling and data leakage prevention strategies, including tooling (e.g. Purview);
Ensure alignment with regulatory and security frameworks (ISO, NIST, AI governance);
Partner with research and business stakeholders to design fit-for-purpose, secure data solutions;
Drive governance, risk and compliance practices across data and information domains; and
Provide technical leadership across projects and contribute to the broader technology roadmap.
About You
 
You are an experienced data professional with a strong blend of architecture, governance and security expertise. You bring a pragmatic, forward-thinking approach to data in complex environments, ideally with exposure to research, healthcare or similarly regulated domains.
 
You will have:

Proven experience across data architecture, governance and data management (5+ years);
Strong knowledge of data classification, labelling and leakage detection approaches;
Experience with frameworks such as ISO 27001, NIST and emerging AI governance standards;
Exposure to cloud platforms (AWS/Azure) and modern data ecosystems; and
Strong stakeholder engagement and the ability to influence across technical and non-technical audiences.
Benefits
 
Working at MCRI offers a range of benefits including:

Access to salary packaging options including entertainment and novated leases;
Flexible working arrangements;
The opportunity to contribute to Australia’s largest child health research institute;
Generous personal leave and the option to purchase additional leave;
Discounted staff parking and excellent public transport access;
Health and wellbeing programs including Employee Assistance Program access; and
Comprehensive paid parental leave for both birth and non-birth parents.
Important Information
 
As this is an exclusive recruitment partnership, all enquiries and communication regarding this position should be directed to u&u Recruitment Partners.
 
Successful applicants will be required to undergo:

A National Police Check; and
A Working With Children Check (to be obtained at the candidate’s own expense).
How to Apply
 
For more information or a confidential discussion please contact Killian O’ Sullivan at u&u at killian.osullivan@uandu.com quoting reference number 44436.
 
At u&u Recruitment Partners, we value diversity, equity and inclusion. We welcome applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, people with diverse cultural and linguistic backgrounds and people with disability.

In response to these requests, we will collaborate closely with you to implement the appropriate adjustments.
 
Additionally, for a barrier-free and inclusive online experience, you can access u&u’s opportunities using accessibility software Recite Me at https://www.uandu.com/jobs.

We’re seeking a Conversational AI Engineer to play a key role in shaping next‑generation customer experiences. This is a rare opportunity to join a greenfield initiative and design intelligent, human‑like voice interactions from the ground up.

In this role, you’ll design, build, and optimise Natural Language IVR and conversational voice experiences using platforms such as Omilia, Nuance, Microsoft, and Google Dialogflow. You’ll work across speech recognition, conversational design, NLU model development, and real‑time speech interpretation to create seamless, intuitive customer journeys.

You’ll collaborate closely with data engineers, data scientists, and contact centre SMEs to enhance speech‑to‑text accuracy, analyse transcription datasets, and continuously improve model performance. If you’re passionate about creating natural, efficient, AI‑driven voice experiences, this role offers the perfect environment to make a meaningful impact.

Key Responsibilities

Design and optimise Natural Language IVR and conversational voice experiences
Build conversational flows, dialogue logic, and voice bot interactions
Improve speech recognition accuracy and interpret real‑time spoken language
Work with transcription datasets to enhance NLU and speech model performance
Integrate conversational AI solutions into contact centre environments
Collaborate with data engineers and data scientists to refine models and reporting
Contribute to customer journey design and contact centre optimisation
Essential Skills & Experience

Experience designing or optimising Natural Language IVR systems
Strong understanding of speech recognition technologies
Ability to build conversational flows and dialogue logic
Familiarity with NLU concepts (intent classification, entity extraction)
Experience analysing speech/transcription datasets
Omilia / NLU platform experience
JavaScript capability for custom logic and integrations
Experience with voice biometrics
Understanding of contact centre operations and customer journey design
Exposure to data science or analytics, particularly speech data
Ability to collaborate with technical teams to improve model accuracy

To be considered for this role, you must be an Australian Citizen and able to obtain Security Clearance.

Magnetica is a medical device engineering and technology company specialising in the development and supply of Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) systems, sub-systems and technologies. Headquartered in Brisbane, Australia, we have offices in Abingdon, near Oxford, UK (Scientific Magnetics Ltd) and Houston, USA (Tecmag, Inc.).

With proven capabilities in R&D, product commercialisation, manufacturing and supply, the company is at a stage of growth and opportunity to provide a unique integrated product offering in the MedTech sector. We are looking for a motivated and detail orientated person to join our team on a permanent basis, based in the Brisbane (Australia) office.

Magnetica is entering an exciting phase as we finalise our first FDA 510(k) submission and complete the transition from final prototypes to commercial products. To support this, we’re looking for a Design Assurance Lead who can ensure that the product, development processes, and documentation all meet regulatory expectations

Role purpose

This role suits someone who enjoys being hands‑on, can move comfortably between strategy and execution, and wants to help shape scalable and compliant processes in a growing medical device company. You’ll work closely with our technical teams to bridge between design & engineering (hardware and software), quality & regulatory, and the FDA’s expectations.

Key elements of the role include:

Ensure design controls are fully implemented and form a compliant DHF

Drive risk management activities including hazard identification, verification of risk controls and ensure traceability

Oversee Verification & Validation strategy to support 510(k) claims for regulatory submission and demonstrating compliance with key standards e.g. IEC 60601

Act as the gatekeeper for design transfer

Ensure documentation is ready for a 510(k) submission

Be the ‘Internal Consultant’ to support teams to unblock issues and resolve ambiguity

Ensure key design and V&V tasks stay on schedule.

Work with Quality to strengthen the QMS for future audits

Required Experience & Capability

Bachelor's Degree in Biomedical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Materials Science, or other relevant technical discipline with demonstrated experience in medical devices.

Knowledge/experience of ISO 13485 Design Controls and applicable market Regulations (MDSAP, MDR).

Demonstrated experience in ISO 14971 aligned risk management processes.

Root cause analysis, CAPA and Non-Conformity leadership and execution.

Involvement in QMS and/or DHF audits.

Experience of the product development process from concept to production.

Experience of Electromechanical systems is desirable.

Experience of working with IEC 60601 is highly desirable.

Experience of working with and set up of ALM and PLM tools is desirable.

Proven problem-solving ability

Approachable personality with strong communication (written and verbal) skills .

Accuracy, attention to detail and task completion.Self-driven, organised and proactive.

Ability to collaborate and support technical and non-technical staff to resolve ambiguity.

This is a unique role supporting Design and Engineering to ensure that our product is safe, effective and compliant with regulatory standards.  Ensuring structured requirements and processes are in place to govern/support the design and development process.

Senior Data Scientist – AI & Machine Learning We’re looking for a Senior Data Scientist to help build and scale the next generation of AI-powered products. This role is for someone who combines deep technical expertise with strong engineering instincts—someone who can take ideas from research through to robust, production-grade systems that deliver real business impact.
You’ll work at the intersection of machine learning, software engineering, and product, partnering closely with Data Scientists, ML Engineers, and cross-functional stakeholders to raise the bar on how AI is designed, built, and deployed.
What You’ll Work On
Productionising AI: Turn cutting-edge ML and Generative AI concepts into scalable, reliable systems used in real-world applications
End-to-End Ownership: Design, build, evaluate, and deploy models across domains such as recommendation systems, pricing optimisation, and customer lifecycle modelling
Experimentation at Scale: Develop rigorous experimentation frameworks to measure model performance and quantify business impact
Modern ML Engineering: Contribute to production-grade pipelines using CI/CD, containerisation, and robust monitoring practices
Cloud-Native AI: Build and deploy solutions using the Vertex AI ecosystem, including pipelines and agent-based architectures
Cross-Functional Impact: Partner with Product and Engineering teams to align technical solutions with commercial outcomes
What You Bring
5+ years of experience delivering machine learning solutions from concept to production
Strong academic background in a quantitative discipline (e.g. Computer Science, Mathematics, Statistics, Engineering)
Hands-on experience in at least two of:
Recommendation systems
Pricing optimisation
Customer lifecycle / growth modelling
Exposure to Generative AI or agent-based systems is a strong plus
Solid grounding in experimentation, statistics, and causal thinking
Advanced Python and SQL skills (experience with BigQuery is highly valued)
Experience working with cloud platforms (GCP preferred) and ML infrastructure
Familiarity with version control, CI/CD, and collaborative software development workflows
Ability to communicate complex ideas clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences
Why Join
Work on high-impact AI problems at scale
Access to unique events and experiences
Flexible leave, wellbeing benefits, and employee support programs
Continuous learning, growth, and career development
A collaborative, inclusive, and high-performing team

Job Title: Senior AI Engineer - South Bank, QLD
Ad Text:
At Flight Centre Travel Group (FCTG) our purpose is to 'open up the world for those who want to see'. Every day, we give people all around the world the opportunity to experience something amazing – travel!  

As the Senior AI Engineer you'll be responsible for designing, building, and operating production grade agentic AI systems, LLM-powered solutions and classical ML workflows that enhance and transform the travel experience across FCTG's Supply business.

You'll take ownership of the full AI engineering lifecycle, from architecture through to deployment and ongoing observability, applying strong engineering standards across MLOps and LLMOps. Working closely with Data Scientists to productionise validated models and with Data, Infrastructure and Security Engineers to integrate AI systems into the broader technology platform, ensuring all AI solutions meet rigorous standards for reliability, scalability, safety, and cost-efficiency.

If you would like to play a key part in raising technical capability across the team by championing best practices, contributing to platform design, and mentoring junior engineers, we encourage you to apply!

 

Lead the design, development, and productionisation of agentic AI workflows, LLM-powered applications, and multi-agent systems
Productionise classical ML models (e.g. classification, regression, time-series forecasting), ensuring they meet production-grade 
standards for reliability, monitoring, and retraining
Own and evolve end-to-end MLOps and LLMOps pipelines including CI/CD, model registry, drift detection, retraining, and automated evaluation
Build and maintain scalable ML infrastructure on Azure and Databricks including feature engineering pipelines and data platform integrations
Architect and build AI agent workflows, endpoints, gateways, and orchestration layers enabling secure tool usage, structured reasoning, and multi-agent collaboration
Deploy and optimise AI workloads on Kubernetes and cloud-native Azure environments
Define and enforce engineering quality standards through code reviews, design reviews, and technical documentation

 

You'll be perfect for the role if you have:

 

Tertiary qualifications in Computer Science, Software Engineering, Data Science or a related field or equivalent practical experience
Proven experience deploying and operating production-grade agentic AI systems, LLM-powered applications, or ML pipelines with commercial impact
Strong Python programming skills and deep knowledge of modern AI/ML frameworks (e.g. LangGraph, LlamaIndex, PyTorch, scikitlearn)
Solid understanding of classical machine learning, including supervised learning (classification, regression), unsupervised learning (clustering, dimensionality reduction), and time-series forecasting, with hands-on experience taking models to production
Strong experience with Azure cloud services (Azure ML, Azure DevOps, Azure Container Services, Azure Function Apps) and Databricks

 

You'll earn brownie points with:

Relevant Azure certifications (e.g. Azure AI Engineer Associate, Azure Solutions Architect, Azure Data Scientist Associate)
Experience with front-end technologies (e.g. TypeScript, React) or infrastructure tooling (e.g. Terraform, Bicep) for building AIpowered interfaces or managing cloud-native deployments.
Experience building data engineering pipelines
Demonstrated contributions to open-source AI projects or published technical work

 

What you'll enjoy:

 

🙋🏾‍♀️Culture: you will be employed by an inclusive company that offers a team culture like no other in the industry - Flight Centre Travel Group Australia is proud to be a certified Great Place To Work® Australia New Zealand Pty Ltd
 
👐🏽 Our People: FCTG is an Equal Opportunity Employer and encourage all suitably qualified applicants to apply, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander People and people from racial, ethnic, or ethno-religious minority groups and people with disability

🔧Access to 'LinkedIn Learning' for ongoing skills development: over 24,500 courses in over 20 languages
 
🏆Exclusive Staff Discounts: Accessible via our employee-only portal with 350+ of Australia’s leading retailers, health and wellness discounts, financial planning advice, employee share plan and more
 
🌏Travel Discounts: Including family and friends - flights, hotels, tours, cruises, travel insurance and more
 
💎Brightness of Future: Career opportunities in a network of brands and businesses across the globe - we promote from within  
 
🥦Corporate Health Discounts: Access exclusive discounts on health insurance plans for you and your family with our partner, BUPA, access to internal gym

About the Company

This organisation is a well-established leader in the utilities sector, delivering critical services to a large and growing customer base. With a strong focus on innovation, sustainability, and future-ready technology, they are investing heavily in data, AI, and advanced analytics.




Their technology function is undergoing a major uplift, with AI positioned as a key driver of smarter decision-making, operational efficiency, and long-term value creation. You'll be joining a business where AI is not just experimental-it's becoming embedded across the enterprise.


About the Role

This is a senior, hands-on leadership role responsible for building and scaling enterprise AI capability. As Lead Data Engineer, you'll design, develop, and deploy AI and machine learning solutions that deliver tangible business outcomes across complex operational environments.




You'll work closely with data leaders, architects, and business stakeholders to identify opportunities and bring high-impact AI use cases to life-from predictive models through to cutting-edge generative AI solutions.




Key responsibilities include:





Leading end-to-end AI/ML solution design, development, and deployment
Driving innovation across NLP, LLMs, and advanced ML techniques
Establishing and scaling MLOps practices (CI/CD, monitoring, lifecycle management)
Building on modern cloud and data platforms (Azure, Databricks)
Embedding AI into enterprise systems to improve efficiency and decision-making
Championing governance, best practice, and responsible AI frameworks
Mentoring engineers and leading cross-functional delivery teams

This role offers a strong mix of strategy and hands-on engineering, with real influence over how AI is adopted across the organisation.


About You

You're an experienced AI/ML engineer with a strong track record of delivering production-ready solutions at scale. You're comfortable operating across both technical and business domains and enjoy turning complex ideas into practical outcomes.




You'll bring:





Strong background in software or data engineering (Python preferred)
Experience with cloud platforms (Azure preferred) and Databricks ecosystem
Deep experience with ML frameworks (TensorFlow, PyTorch, scikit-learn)
Solid MLOps capability, including model deployment and monitoring
Exposure to generative AI, LLMs, and modern AI frameworks (e.g. LangChain)
Proven ability to deliver AI solutions that drive measurable business value
Strong stakeholder engagement and leadership experience

Recognised as one of the world’s largest IT and business consulting firms, CGI partners with clients across Australia’s public and private sectors to solve complex business challenges through technology and innovation.

This senior leadership role calls for a leader who can combine executive presence with a collaborative, inclusive approach, bringing both strategic vision and hands-on expertise to guide meaningful outcomes.

You will partner closely with clients to design and implement enterprise-grade AI and data strategies—translating business vision into data-driven outcomes that create measurable value. As a trusted advisor to senior executives, you will bring confidence, empathy, and influence, shaping conversations and building strong, lasting relationships.

A key expectation of this role is the ability to define, build, and scale AI offerings, contributing to CGI’s growth through innovative, practical, and human-centred solutions. You will also champion thought leadership, helping clients and teams navigate the evolving AI landscape with clarity and purpose.

You will enjoy flexible working, a supportive and inclusive culture, and the opportunity to contribute to a team where innovation, leadership, and growth are part of everyday work.

Your future role and responsibilities:

Lead enterprise-scale AI and data transformation programs across strategy, architecture, governance, and delivery.

Partner with C-suite stakeholders to shape AI-driven business outcomes and long-term value.

Bring a hands-on, supportive approach to solution design, architecture validation, and delivery guidance.

Demonstrate authentic executive presence, building trust and influencing strategic conversations.

Develop and implement enterprise data and AI strategies, including governance and ethical AI frameworks.

Shape and deliver AI and ML initiatives, such as predictive analytics, generative AI adoption, automation, and intelligent decision support.

Build and scale AI offerings, including reusable frameworks and industry solutions.

Contribute to business growth through client engagement, opportunity shaping, and relationship building.

Mentor and uplift teams, fostering a collaborative, inclusive, and high-performing culture.


Experience required to be successful in this role:

Proven leadership in enterprise AI and data transformation programs.

Strong understanding of data architecture, engineering, governance, and cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, Databricks, Snowflake, GCP).

Demonstrated hands-on experience in AI/ML solution design or implementation.

Expertise in AI technologies (machine learning, NLP, computer vision, GenAI).

Strong executive presence with the ability to influence and build trusted relationships.

Experience in developing and scaling consulting offerings and go-to-market solutions.

Excellent business development and stakeholder engagement skills.

Strong leadership, communication, and mentoring capabilities, with a focus on empowering others.

Recognised as one of the world’s largest IT and business consulting firms, CGI has offices across Australia, supporting local public and private sector clients to solve real business problems.

We are looking to hire a Data Modeller who will focus on translating complex business requirements into clear, scalable, conceptual, logical, and physical data models that support analytics, reporting, and operational needs. Working closely with data engineers, analysts, and business stakeholders, the Data Modeller ensures that data is well-organized, standardized, and aligned with governance and quality frameworks to enable efficient data-driven decision making across the organization.

Flexible work is available including hybrid work from client’s site at Port Melbourne  

Your future role and responsibilities:

Contribute to the design, development, and continuous refinement of conceptual, logical, and physical data models within the Data Transformation team.

Create and maintain SQL DDL scripts, along with detailed mapping and ETL documentation, to support Data Engineers in constructing and loading data models.

When required, carry out reverse engineering of existing data models from databases or SQL code to ensure alignment with current architecture and standards.

Develop and update business documentation, including process maps, taxonomies, and ontology diagrams, to provide clarity and traceability of data flows.

Maintain a strong emphasis on conceptual and business data modelling, ensuring that structures align with organisational objectives.

Build and scale a healthcare platform using AI-first engineering

Hybrid role


Note: This role is focused on relational database architecture — schema design, optimisation, and large-scale migrations — not data science, analytics pipelines, or ML/AI model training.


At vital.ly, our engineering scope is broad and complex.

Externally, our proprietary customer platform supports over 14,000 healthcare practitioners. It enables wholesale ordering for clinic dispensaries, advanced prescribing workflows with built-in clinical safety features, and digital prescriptions sent directly to patients for online fulfilment - all powered by Australia’s most comprehensive complementary medicine reference tools.

Internally, the proprietary platform is the backbone of our distribution business, turning prescriptions into deliveries. It provides customised workflows and tools that manage practitioner access, inventory, orders, and reference content as well as protect patient data - making this ecosystem uniquely powerful.

Connecting every part, internal and external APIs ensure seamless integration, while AWS infrastructure delivers security, resilience, and scalability.

As we evolve the platform, we are undertaking a critical transformation of our data architecture — moving from a legacy MySQL schema to a structured, scalable PostgreSQL environment, while embedding AI-first engineering practices into how we build.




Who this role is for:

You've spent years inside relational databases — arguing about index cardinality, diagnosing query plans, and adding foreign keys to production tables without downtime. You've migrated between database engines and you know the hardest part is never the data — it's the implicit assumptions in the application code.

This is a principal engineer role from day one. Your first major workstream is owning the MySQL → PostgreSQL migration end-to-end. Once that lands, your focus shifts to platform architecture, data modelling, and technical leadership alongside the Head of Engineering.

You think in terms of systems, not just features. Data integrity, performance, and long-term maintainability matter to you, and you take a structured approach to solving problems that others may consider too complex or risky.

You are comfortable working independently and taking ownership of a critical workstream. When something isn’t ready, you can clearly explain why — and back that up with sound technical reasoning.

We build with AI-assisted development tools (Claude, agentic workflows).

This isn't an ML role - we expect you to be an efficiency multiplier, designing workflows and guardrails for the team and pushing the boundaries of AI-amplified engineering. If you're still hand-writing every line and plan to keep it that way, this isn't the right fit.




The Responsibilities


Phase 1 (Schema Formalisation & Migration)

Design and operate agentic AI workflows (tools, prompts, guardrails) to accelerate schema auditing and migration tasks.

Audit the full MySQL schema, including tables, columns, indexes, implicit relationships, and type/charset/collation inconsistencies.

Add foreign keys and constraints to a live production database without downtime using Laravel’s migration tooling.

Resolve data inconsistencies, including orphaned rows, type mismatches, and ambiguous columns, in collaboration with founding engineers.

Plan and execute the MySQL to PostgreSQL migration end-to-end.

Document all schema changes with migration files, clear rationale, and rollback plans.

Coordinate migration sequencing and provide clear, consistent progress reporting to the team.


Phase 2 (Platform Expansion)

Partner with the Head of Engineering to uphold high standards of data architecture and optimisation across the platform.

Define and refine AI agent rules, roles, and guardrails to support AI-first engineering practices.

Review merge requests and authorise production releases.

Own the search architecture, including tsvector FTS, postGIS and pgvector embeddings.

Design and build platform features on the migrated architecture.

Own modules end-to-end across the service layer, APIs, and frontend where required.

Optimise query performance, define indexing strategies, and guide ongoing data modelling across the platform.

Shape technical direction and architectural decisions as the product evolves.




Requirements

Must haves:

Deep expertise in relational database internals: storage engines, locking behaviour, transaction isolation, index internals, and collation/character set optimisation. You should be able to explain why a query is slow, not just that it is.

Proven experience executing large-scale data migrations (millions of rows) with batching, progress tracking, validation, and safe rollback strategies.

Architecture-level engineering experience, including API, modular monoliths, and contract-driven design.

Experience with AI-assisted development workflows and a genuine enthusiasm for pushing their boundaries - including reviewing and validating AI-generated output for production quality.

This is a permanent, full-time role and requires unrestricted and ongoing work rights in Australia.

Nice to have:

Experience with CDC processes and MySQL binlog-based replication or change data capture mechanisms.

Experience working with pgvector for embedding storage and similarity search.

Experience optimising PostgreSQL full-text search using tsvector and related indexing strategies.

Production experience with AWS infrastructure and services, including ECS, RDS, CloudFront, Secrets Manager, EC2, and CloudWatch.

Experience in healthcare or other regulated environments.

Hands-on experience with PHP/Laravel

Experience building custom developer tooling - MCP servers, CLI tools, workflow automation.




About vital.ly

vital.ly is the infrastructure behind sustainable clinical practice. Trusted by over 14,000 practitioners across Australia, vital.ly integrates three pillars of infrastructure — technology platform, distribution capability, and practitioner expertise — to transform how clinics prescribe and dispense complementary medicines.

Through vital.ly, practitioners can access wholesale ordering from leading brands, dispense prescriptions digitally, and use advanced prescribing tools with built-in clinical safety tools. Every order is fulfilled through our own dedicated warehouse, with fulfilment operations designed for speed, accuracy, and scale. Behind it all is a team of qualified practitioners and experienced professionals who provide the support and guidance needed to practice with confidence.

Joining vital.ly means becoming part of a business that empowers practitioners, improves patient outcomes, and drives innovation in complementary medicine.

Job Title: Machine Learning Engineer - Deep Learning Specialist (Computer Vision)
Ad Text:
Company Description


Property intelligence is reshaping how the world understands the built environment, and Nearmap is driving that. We put powerful aerial imagery, AI-driven analytics, and geospatial tools into the hands of the people who plan, build, insure, and govern the places we all live and work. Our technology turns property uncertainty into decisive action, and our culture brings out the best in the people who build it.

Job Description


We are recruiting a hands-on Machine Learning Engineer with a passion for R&D and strong expertise in applying deep learning techniques to practical computer vision applications. You will work with an incredibly passionate and talented team of Data Scientists and Machine Learning Engineers, and your work will have a real impact on Nearmap products. You will train novel deep learning models that leverage our rich data sets, multiple dates of high-resolution imagery, multi-angle source imagery and 3D textured mesh. You will collaborate closely with other team members to solve various 2D and 3D vision tasks by selecting the model architecture, designing training and evaluation methodologies, generating a suitable dataset, and optimising models for deployment. The released models will run on millions of square kilometres of Nearmap imagery.

Experience in applying deep learning techniques to commercial use cases and non-academic problems is highly valued. We are committed to software best practices including infrastructure as code, GitOps, CI/CD, and as much automation as makes sense. A strong background in 3D modelling is advantageous in this role.

Key Responsibilities

Design and scope greenfield machine learning projects in collaboration with the team
Develop and deliver end-to-end solutions to complex technical problems
Train and optimize deep learning models using our extensive multi-temporal, multi-angle, and 3D imagery datasets
Participate in technical discussions, code reviews, and knowledge sharing sessions

Qualifications


Mandatory Skills

Programming/Tech Environments: Ability to code in scientific Python, using a Linux environment, and Git for source control
Machine Learning: Strong grasp of machine learning fundamentals (regularisation, hyperparameter optimisation, validation methods), and recent AI advancements
Scientific Approach: Follow the scientific method of formulating hypotheses, and applying statistical tests to validate them
Deep Learning: Applying modern artificial neural networks to solve machine learning problem
Tertiary Qualifications Formal education in a field related to computer science, machine learning, deep learning and AI.

Highly Desirable Skills

Domain Knowledge – Computer Vision: Working on Machine Learning problems applied to image data
Domain Knowledge – 3D Reconstruction: Experience with 3D computer vision, photogrammetry, structure-from-motion, or related technologies
Software Engineering: Working on shared codebases to produce production quality code
Cloud Computing: Working on AWS or GCP using distributed virtual machines, Docker containers, etc.
GP-GPU: Using GPUs to accelerate scientific computing
Scale: Working with large data sets, where data sets don't fit into memory, and require multiple nodes to compute efficiently

Personal Attributes

Pragmatism: While extensive knowledge of ML theory is highly valued, we prioritize pragmatic solutions that work over elaborate theory when shipping products
Collaboration: Data science is a team sport—communicate well, share knowledge, and be open to taking on ideas from anyone in the team
Attention to detail: Thoroughness in model development, testing, and documentation

This role is based in Sydney, Australia but also open to candidates from the east coast of Australia working remotely.

Please note: Visa sponsorship is not available for this role. Applicants must have permanent rights to work in Australia.
 

Additional Information


Some of our benefits

Nearmap takes a holistic approach to our employees’ emotional, physical and financial wellness. Some of our current benefits include:

Quarterly wellbeing day off - Four additional days off annually for your 'YOU' Days
Wellbeing and technology allowance
Annual flu vaccinations
Hybrid flexibility for this role 
Nearmap subscription (of course!)
Stocked kitchen with access to all the snacks you need
In-office lunch every Tuesday and Thursday at our Sydney CBD office
Showers available for anyone cycling to work or lunchtime gym-goers!

Working at Nearmap

We move fast, we care about craft, and we're proud of what we're building. If you're energised by turning hard problems into real-world impact, we'd love to meet you

Company Description


Property intelligence is reshaping how the world understands the built environment, and Nearmap is driving that. Job Description


Transform aerial capture systems with cutting-edge embedded solutions

Nearmap is seeking a Senior Embedded Systems Engineer to join our innovative Capture Systems team. Work at the intersection of embedded systems and aerial imaging technology, developing real-time solutions that power our next-generation capture systems. You'll contribute across the full embedded systems lifecycle- from R&D through to production and manufacturing.

Work on sophisticated embedded systems that power our aerial imagery capture platforms used worldwide. You'll be integral to developing real-time solutions for current and future capture system components, contributing across the entire lifecycle from initial R&D through qualification, production, and manufacturing. This is a rare opportunity to work on complex mechatronic systems where precision and reliability are critical.

What You'll Be Working On

Real-Time Embedded Systems: Specify, develop, document, and qualify embedded systems solutions for aerial capture platforms, with a focus on bare-metal development and deterministic performance
High-Precision Motor Control: Research, develop, and qualify advanced motor control solutions that demand ultra-high precision for aerial imagery capture systems
Hardware-Software Integration: Interface embedded software with sophisticated electronic hardware systems, ensuring seamless operation in real-world aerial environments
Verification & Validation: Develop and execute comprehensive testing strategies, both cross-platform and on target hardware, to ensure system reliability and performance
Control Systems Development: Design and implement linear and non-linear feedback controllers for electro-mechanical applications, combining theoretical modelling with practical implementation
Cross-Disciplinary Collaboration: Work closely with multi-disciplinary Technology and Capture Systems teams, contributing to knowledge sharing and driving innovation across the organisation

Who You Are

Excellent communicator with strong verbal, written, and visual communication skills
Positive self-starter who thrives in collaborative, team-oriented environments
Comfortable working in fast-paced, dynamic settings where priorities can shift
High emotional intelligence with the ability to work effectively across different teams and disciplines
Detail-oriented problem solver who can balance theoretical knowledge with practical implementation
Passionate about embedded systems engineering and eager to tackle complex technical challenges

Qualifications


Essential:

8+ years of engineering experience in embedded systems with an emphasis on bare metal development 

Desired: 10+ years; experienced in bare-metal embedded software; familiar with ARM Cortex-M7 architecture 

Strong proficiency in C++ and Python; advantageous: C 

Real-time/deterministic software development 

Interfacing software to electronic hardware systems 

Verification and validation of embedded software: cross-platform and on target hardware

Highly Regarded:

10+ years embedded systems experience
ARM Cortex-M7 architecture expertise
Ultra high-precision mechatronic systems background
Control systems theory and practical implementation experience

This is a genuine opportunity to own greenfield platform work at serious scale. Nearmap is one of the top three AWS users in the APAC region. You'll sit between traditional DevOps and the new world of AI engineering — which means this role suits someone with a strong back-end and platform engineering foundation who is ready to grow their career into AI, or who is already making that transition. You'll own projects end to end — from architecture and design through to implementation, delivery, and production operations. That kind of trust and autonomy is real here, not a recruitment line.

Engaging directly with ML tooling vendors including AWS, GCP, Weights & Biases, Confluent, and Arize AI

The teams depending on your platform include Model R&D, Computer Vision, Insurance Data Science, Agentic AI, and AI Map Data.

Production experience building and operating Kubernetes-based platforms (EKS/GKE) at scale
High-scale, high-concurrency API development and operation in production environments
Real operational mindset: you've managed systems with SLAs and SLOs and stayed close through incidents
Strong DevOps fundamentals — CI/CD, infrastructure-as-code, deployment pipelines

Ray Serve or equivalent real-time model serving experience
LLM or generative AI platform work in production (beyond PoC)
ML observability tooling — Arize AI, Evidently, Prometheus/Grafana
Infrastructure-as-code with Terraform
Experience at terabyte or petabyte scale
GPU/CPU cost optimisation

A degree in computer science, engineering, mathematics, or a related field is welcome — equivalent practical experience is equally valued.

About Us

Beck Water is a growing national water infrastructure business committed to solving water challenges with reliable systems, transparent service, and local expertise. As we grow, we're investing in smart technology to work smarter — and we're looking for someone to lead that charge. This role sits within Beck Water however carries the potential to extend across our broader group of companies, giving the right person significant scope to grow their impact.

⚠️ This role is based on-premises on the Gold Coast. Please do not apply if you are not currently living close to the Gold Coast — remote and hybrid arrangements are not available for this position.

The Role

This is a hands-on, exploratory role for someone who gets excited about finding inefficiencies and fixing them with practical AI solutions. You won't be handed a list of tasks — you'll spend your first months understanding how we operate, then design strategy (including things like security polices for AI, in conjuction with management) and deliver automation tools that make a real difference: smarter reporting, email workflows, task management, chatbots, and more.

You'll work primarily with Claude as your core tool — and we mean properly across it: Claude Code, Cowork, and hands-on vibe coding of real applications. You'll remain open to integrating other platforms where they best fit the problem.

What You'll Do

Months 1–3: Embed yourself in the business. Shadow teams, map processes and policies, and identify where time is being lost.

Months 4–6: Deliver weekly quick wins — automations and AI tools that teams can start using immediately.

Beyond: Own and lead larger transformation projects across Beck Water and potentially other companies within our group.

What You Bring

You're fully down the rabbit hole with Claude — actively using Claude Code, Cowork, and Claude-powered workflows as part of your daily toolkit, not just experimenting

Demonstrated experience vibe coding and shipping real applications — we want to see what you've built

Hands-on experience with AI workflow and automation tools — you know when to use which tool and why

Practical experience with low-code integration platforms — minimum 2+ years

A minimum of 1–2 years hands-on experience with LLM platforms, with Claude as the preferred benchmark

A degree in IT, Computer Science, or a related field — or equivalent hands-on experience

Genuine curiosity about how businesses work and where technology can improve them

The ability to translate technical ideas into clear recommendations for non-technical stakeholders

A bias toward action and a track record of getting things shipped

Openness to working across different business contexts as the role evolves

Australian work rights are required for this position

What We Offer

A real seat at the table — your ideas will be heard and implemented

The opportunity to shape how an entire group of businesses uses AI, from the ground up

Variety — exposure to different industries and challenges as you grow in the role

A collaborative team that values initiative and curiosity
